Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Effect of building direction on the microstructure and tensile properties of Ti-48Al-2Cr-2Nb alloy additively manufactured by electron beam melting

Composition_Info.Note: Al loss of approximately 2 at% due to evaporation during EBM process. Ti balance computed as 100 - sum of other elements.

Process_Text.original: Ti-48Al-2Cr-2Nb alloy powder (average diameter ~100 um) fabricated using Arcam A2X EBM system (Arcam AB, Moelndal, Sweden). Electron beam scanned in zigzag pattern with 90 degree rotation between layers. Layer thickness 90 um. Pre-heating temperature 1060 degC before melting. Acceleration voltage 60 kV. Building direction angle theta = 0 degrees between building direction and cylindrical axis.
Process_Text.simplified: EBM of Ti-48Al-2Cr-2Nb powder using Arcam A2X system; 60 kV acceleration voltage; 90 um layer thickness; 1060 degC pre-heating; zigzag beam pattern with 90 deg rotation per layer; theta=0 deg build orientation.

Process_Info.Equipment: Arcam A2X EBM system (Arcam AB, Mölndal, Sweden)

Microstructure_Text.original: Unique layered microstructure composed of duplex-like regions and gamma bands. Duplex-like region: predominantly lamellar-structured gamma/alpha2 fine grains and fine equiaxed gamma grains. Gamma bands: coarser equiaxed gamma phase grains forming chains perpendicular to building direction. Layers arranged perpendicular to building direction. Layer thickness: duplex-like ~60 um, gamma bands ~30 um. Total layer thickness ~90 um corresponds to powder layer thickness. Beta phase exists mostly near surface region.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Layered microstructure with alternating duplex-like regions (fine lamellar gamma/alpha2 + fine equiaxed gamma, ~60 um thick) and gamma bands (coarser equiaxed gamma, ~30 um thick). Two-phase gamma + alpha2 matrix.

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.XRD: Philips X-pert Pro; gamma-TiAl (L10) and alpha2-Ti3Al (D019) phases identified; minor beta (bcc) near surface
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.SEM-EBSD: Layered microstructure observation; beta phases confirmed near surface region
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.TEM: Lamellar structure analysis; orientation relationship {111}gamma // (0001)alpha2, <110>gamma // <11-20>alpha2 confirmed

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at ~298 K (room temperature); theta=0 deg loading orientation; strain rate 1.67e-4 s^-1
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ~0.8 x 1.5 mm cross section, 5 mm gauge length
Properties_Info.Note: Highest yield strength at room temperature among the three orientations

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 800 degC; theta=0 deg loading orientation; strain rate 1.67e-4 s^-1
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ~0.8 x 1.5 mm cross section, 5 mm gauge length

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at ~298 K (room temperature); theta=0 deg loading orientation; strain rate 1.67e-4 s^-1
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ~0.8 x 1.5 mm cross section, 5 mm gauge length
Properties_Info.Note: Lowest elongation at room temperature among orientations; interface perpendicular to loading axis suppresses shear deformation

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 800 degC; theta=0 deg loading orientation; strain rate 1.67e-4 s^-1
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ~0.8 x 1.5 mm cross section, 5 mm gauge length

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 800 degC; theta=0 deg loading orientation; strain rate 1.67e-4 s^-1
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ~0.8 x 1.5 mm cross section, 5 mm gauge length
Properties_Info.Note: Ultimate tensile strength exceeds 350 MPa at 800 degC

Composition_Info.Note: Al loss of approximately 2 at% due to evaporation during EBM process. Ti balance computed as 100 - sum of other elements.
